# Building on HyperBEAM with `ao`

The guides for building applications on `HyperBEAM` and interacting with `ao` processes have been moved to the [AO Cookbook](https://cookbook_ao.arweave.net) to provide a centralized resource for developers.

### Learning the Basics of `ao`

For a foundational understanding of `ao` and how to get started building processes, the AO Cookbook is the best place to start.

[**Get Started with `ao`**](https://cookbook_ao.arweave.net/welcome/getting-started.html)

### Migrating from `legacynet`

If you are migrating processes from `legacynet`, the cookbook provides guides on leveraging new `HyperBEAM` features, such as exposing process state directly over HTTP to replace `dryrun` calls.

[**View the Migration Guide**](https://cookbook_ao.arweave.net/guides/migrating-to-hyperbeam/exposing-process-state.html)

### Using `aos` and `aoconnect` on HyperBEAM

For guides on using the `aos` command-line tool or the `aoconnect` library to interact with processes running on `HyperBEAM`, please see the relevant cookbook pages.

*   [**Using `aos` with HyperBEAM**](https://cookbook_ao.arweave.net/guides/migrating-to-hyperbeam/aos-with-hyperbeam.html)
*   [**Using `aoconnect` with HyperBEAM**](https://cookbook_ao.arweave.net/guides/migrating-to-hyperbeam/ao-connect.html)
